rulings.Case Title: JOSE INIGUEZ-FRANCO VS. ELEVATOR, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protetive Order Finding The Number Of Specially Prepared Interrgoatoriesa Nd Reqeusts For Admission Propounded Unwarranted Contratry To Defts
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 24, 2011, line 1. PLAINTIFF JOSE INIGUEZ-FRANCO'S Motion For Protective Order Finding The Number Of Specially Prepared Interrgoatories and Reqeusts For Admission Propounded Unwarranted Contrary To Defendants. Plaintiff's motion for a Protective Order finding the number of specifically prepared interrogatories and requests for admissions unwarranted is granted. Sanctions in the amount of $1300 is awarded to plaintiff. Defendant and defendant's attorney shall remit payment of the sanctions to plaintiff's counsel within 15 days of the entry of this order. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: MANOLITA G. ARROYO VS. BOARD OF TRUSTEES, SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 24, 2011, line 10, DEFENDANTS BOARD OF TRUSTEES, SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE, CLARA STARR DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 3RD AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION. AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO ALLEGE THAT SHE WAS ENGAGED IN PROTECTED ACTIVITY. CUMMINGS V. CIVIL SERVICE COMM., (1995) 40 CAL.APP.4TH 1643. AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO ALLEGE ANY HARRASSING CONDUCT. RENO V. BAIRD, (1998) 18 CAL.4TH 640, 645. =(302/LMG)
